result:
1. Trusts client `X-Forwarded-For` for rate-limit keying — `req.headers['x-forwarded-for']` in diff. Exploit: attacker rotates XFF per request to bypass per-IP limits (recurrence of 2025-09 SEV1 brute-force). Fix: route IP through `trustedClientIp(req)` helper (Section 2 rule 2); `req.socket.remoteAddress` authoritative unless XFF came from proxy in `config/trusted_proxies.json`. Verify: `no-direct-xff-read` lint rule must flag; add unit test asserting spoofed XFF with untrusted socket peer keys off socket IP.
2. No `EXPIRE` on `INCR` — unbounded key `rl:…` (recurrence of 2025-11 SEV2 Redis OOM). Fix: wrap in `MULTI` pairing `INCR` + `EXPIRE(key, 90)` per Section 4 pattern. Verify: integration test asserting TTL>0 after first INCR; `no-unbounded-redis-key` custom ESLint rule must flag this call site.
3. Missing route prefix, method, and region in key — `rl:${ip}` only. Impact: all routes/methods share one bucket (limit math wrong); cross-region collision (recurrence of 2025-12 SEV2). Fix: key `rl:{region}:{ip}:{route_prefix}:{minute_bucket}` per Section 4; pull limit from `config/rate_limits.json` by route+method.
4. No minute bucket — `Date.now()` not floored to window. Impact: counter never rolls over cleanly; with TTL present it drifts, without TTL it grows forever. Fix: append `Math.floor(Date.now()/60000)` to key per Section 4.
5. No limit check or 429 response — `incr` result discarded. Impact: limiter counts but never throttles (silent no-op; defeats purpose of the middleware). Fix: compare `count > limit`, return RFC 7807 `problem+json` body with `type:/errors/rate-limited`, `status:429`, `retry_after`.
6. Unhandled Redis error — bare `await redis.incr` with no try/catch or retry. Impact: transient Redis blip becomes `5xx` at edge instead of fail-open or retried per `src/util/retry.ts`; also bumps `atlas_edge_rate_limit_total{action=errored}` unemitted. Fix: wrap in try/catch, decide policy (fail-open is current norm), emit metric, delegate via `next(err)` only on unrecoverable.

raw_payload.result:
1. Trusts client `X-Forwarded-For` for rate-limit keying — `req.headers['x-forwarded-for']` in diff. Exploit: attacker rotates XFF per request to bypass per-IP limits (recurrence of 2025-09 SEV1 brute-force). Fix: route IP through `trustedClientIp(req)` helper (Section 2 rule 2); `req.socket.remoteAddress` authoritative unless XFF came from proxy in `config/trusted_proxies.json`. Verify: `no-direct-xff-read` lint rule must flag; add unit test asserting spoofed XFF with untrusted socket peer keys off socket IP.
2. No `EXPIRE` on `INCR` — unbounded key `rl:…` (recurrence of 2025-11 SEV2 Redis OOM). Fix: wrap in `MULTI` pairing `INCR` + `EXPIRE(key, 90)` per Section 4 pattern. Verify: integration test asserting TTL>0 after first INCR; `no-unbounded-redis-key` custom ESLint rule must flag this call site.
3. Missing route prefix, method, and region in key — `rl:${ip}` only. Impact: all routes/methods share one bucket (limit math wrong); cross-region collision (recurrence of 2025-12 SEV2). Fix: key `rl:{region}:{ip}:{route_prefix}:{minute_bucket}` per Section 4; pull limit from `config/rate_limits.json` by route+method.
4. No minute bucket — `Date.now()` not floored to window. Impact: counter never rolls over cleanly; with TTL present it drifts, without TTL it grows forever. Fix: append `Math.floor(Date.now()/60000)` to key per Section 4.
5. No limit check or 429 response — `incr` result discarded. Impact: limiter counts but never throttles (silent no-op; defeats purpose of the middleware). Fix: compare `count > limit`, return RFC 7807 `problem+json` body with `type:/errors/rate-limited`, `status:429`, `retry_after`.
6. Unhandled Redis error — bare `await redis.incr` with no try/catch or retry. Impact: transient Redis blip becomes `5xx` at edge instead of fail-open or retried per `src/util/retry.ts`; also bumps `atlas_edge_rate_limit_total{action=errored}` unemitted. Fix: wrap in try/catch, decide policy (fail-open is current norm), emit metric, delegate via `next(err)` only on unrecoverable.
